topic: Reading a Biography: Commander Abhilash Tomy
title: Commander Abhilash Tomy's Incredible Journey and Parenthesis

questions.question: What does "circumnavigation" mean?
options: a non-fiction story about someone else's life.
an exact copy of something, like a model boat.
travel all the way around something, like the Earth.
unable to move, often because of an injury.
questions.correct_answer: travel all the way around something, like the Earth.
questions.explanation: "circumnavigation" means: travel all the way around something, like the Earth.

questions.question: What does "replica" mean?
options: a non-fiction story about someone else's life.
travel all the way around something, like the Earth.
unable to move, often because of an injury.
an exact copy of something, like a model boat.
questions.correct_answer: an exact copy of something, like a model boat.
questions.explanation: "replica" means: an exact copy of something, like a model boat.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Commander Abhilash Tomy completed an ______ solo journey around the world.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Commander Abhilash Tomy completed an amazing solo journey around the world."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Brackets are used for parenthesis, which adds extra, non-essential information to a sentence.
It has no special meaning in writing.
A biography tells the factual story of a real person's life, written by someone else.
Commander Abhilash Tomy completed an amazing solo journey around the world.
questions.correct_answer: A biography tells the factual story of a real person's life, written by someone else.
questions.explanation: Correct! "A biography tells the factual story of a real person's life, written by someone else." is one of the key things to remember.

questions.question: What does "immobilised" mean?
options: an exact copy of something, like a model boat.
travel all the way around something, like the Earth.
a non-fiction story about someone else's life.
unable to move, often because of an injury.
questions.correct_answer: unable to move, often because of an injury.
questions.explanation: "immobilised" means: unable to move, often because of an injury.
